evidence: Each frame has a single load-bearing element: the retrieval card in F1, the you/role comparison in F2, the emerging guess in F3, the outlined candidate with rose flag in F4, and the closing card in F5-F6.
The busiest frame (F2) shows two labeled columns and one dial, well under the four-novel-element budget.
Labels sit on their objects: 'YOU'/'THE ROLE' head their columns, 'candidate' sits under the vector, 'size, not fit' beside the flag, 'score: 96' by the dial.
All text functions as card prompts, column headers, or annotations of visible referents (flag, vector, dial).
Card prompts, column entries, and readouts are all rendered at comfortably readable size.
The cards, columns, vector, dial, and extensions chip all sit inside the frame with margins.
No text or labels overlap; the 'size, not fit' annotation and 'score: 96' readout sit clear of the vector and dial.
The candidate vector is periwinkle blue, the dial needle and score readout are lilac, and the unearned-win flag is rose — consistent with token semantics.
The stray marks in F3 read as the incipient build of the candidate vector and angle arc rather than decoration; every element carries propositional role.

The sequence delivers the intent: retrieval card, you/role columns pooling into a lilac dial, a guess card with outlined candidate and hidden score, then the candidate stretching longer with lilac score climbing to 96, a rose flag marking the unearned win, and the closing walk-home card with an extensions-menu chip.
Annotations touch their referents: 'size, not fit' beside the flag, 'candidate' under the vector, and the extensions chip near its pointing arrow.
Across frames the candidate builds from a fragment (F3) into a full outlined vector with flag (F4), reading as a continuous construction in reasoning order.
Generous negative space and clear top-to-bottom reading order across all six frames.
